summary.title: Prevent mobile docs horizontal overflow
summary.what: Updated the shared site stylesheet so card-like grid items can shrink inside narrow viewports and docs code blocks scroll horizontally inside their own pre containers.
summary.why: The docs pages were expanding the whole mobile viewport because long command snippets forced the docs grid cards to their min-content width. The fix keeps the page viewport at 390px while preserving readable long commands through local code-block scrolling.
summary.user_goal: Fix the severe horizontal overflow on the mobile docs page and publish the change through the Mainline workflow.

decisions.chose: Add min-width: 0 to the repeated card/grid article rule used by docs cards and related grids.
decisions.rationale: CSS grid items otherwise keep their min-content width, so long code snippets can force the entire page wider than the viewport. Letting the item shrink addresses the layout cause without changing content.

decisions.chose: Constrain docs pre elements to their container and enable overflow-x: auto.
decisions.rationale: Long install/protocol commands remain intact and readable, but their overflow is contained inside the code block instead of becoming page-level horizontal scroll.

rejected.alternative: Rewrite or shorten the docs command snippets
rejected.reason: The content was correct; the bug was layout containment, not command length.

review_notes: Validated with npm run build and headless Chrome 390px checks for /docs/, /zh/docs/, and /es/docs/: documentElement.scrollWidth and bodyScrollWidth are 390px on each page.
This work does not resolve the existing copy-review, hero-copy, or production-domain follow-ups surfaced by Mainline.

behavioral_changes: Mobile docs cards no longer expand the document width when they contain long command snippets.
Docs code blocks can scroll horizontally within the pre element on narrow screens.
